behavior_surfaces: Claude Code local transcript token aggregation
desktop Activity settings Token usage summary
desktop activity stats API response shape
state_contracts: Local Claude Code transcript records remain read-only and are not mutated.
dailyModelTokens keeps the existing total-token semantic including cache read and cache creation tokens.
dailyTokenBreakdown exposes per-day input, output, cache read, and cache creation token categories for UI explanation.
stats-cache.json schema is v6; pre-v6 caches are rejected and recomputed because token categories cannot be reconstructed safely.
ActivityStats dailyTokenBreakdown is optional on the desktop API type for backward compatibility with older server responses.
verification: cd desktop && bun run test ActivitySettings.test.tsx: passed 2 tests after red-first regression
bun test src\utils\__tests__\stats.test.ts: passed 6 tests / 19 expects
bun run check:persistence-upgrade: passed server persistent JSON migrations and desktop localStorage migrations
bun run check:desktop: passed 96 test files / 828 tests plus desktop build/typecheck
bun run check:server: blocked by existing expired quarantine entries before relevant checks
bun run check:coverage: blocked by existing expired quarantine entries before coverage calculation
verification_evidence: Read-only replay of local Claude Code transcripts for 2026-06-02 produced 246,202,200 total tokens: input 7,426,293, output 777,315, cache read 237,998,592.
ActivitySettings regression asserts daily cache contribution appears in the Today summary detail.
stats regression asserts dailyTokenBreakdown preserves token categories and v6 cache loading keeps the breakdown intact.
stats cache regression asserts pre-v6 caches are invalidated instead of migrated with inferred category data.
known_unknowns: Live desktop screenshot/browser smoke was not run in this pass.
Full verify/coverage could not complete until expired quarantine entries are reviewed.
Existing unrelated dirty files were present and preserved.
confidence_notes: The user-visible anomaly was reproduced from local read-only transcript data before implementation.
The fix is additive at the API/UI observation boundary and keeps existing total-token aggregation semantics stable.
The persistence schema change has both an old-cache invalidation test and a current-cache load test.
user_decisions: User reported that Today 230M tokens looked implausible and asked whether the Token usage calculation was wrong.

boundary.description: Token usage dashboard explanation/debug fix; no local transcript mutation and no provider/runtime token metering changes.
excludes: Claude Code CLI transcript file format changes
workflow UI compacting changes from the previous task
